and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage options\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\tapiserveroptions \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/options\"\n\tcomponentbaseconfig \"k8s.io/component-base/config\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/logs\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2/ktesting\"\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/kube-scheduler/config/v1\"\n\tkubeschedulerconfig \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/latest\"\n\tconfigtesting \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/testing\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/testing/defaults\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/names\"\n\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n)\n\nfunc TestSchedulerOptions(t *testing.T) {\n\t// temp dir\n\ttmpDir, err := os.MkdirTemp(\"\", \"scheduler-options\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\tdefer os.RemoveAll(tmpDir)\n\n\t// record the username requests were made with\n\tusername := \"\"\n\t// https server\n\tserver := httptest.NewTLSServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\t\tusername, _, _ = req.BasicAuth()\n\t\tif username == \"\" {\n\t\t\tusername = \"none, tls\"\n\t\t}\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(200)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`ok`))\n\t}))\n\tdefer server.Close()\n\t// http server\n\tinsecureserver := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\t\tusername, _, _ = req.BasicAuth()\n\t\tif username == \"\" {\n\t\t\tusername = \"none, http\"\n\t\t}\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(200)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`ok`))\n\t}))\n\tdefer insecureserver.Close()\n\n\t// config file and kubeconfig\n\tconfigFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"scheduler.yaml\")\n\tconfigKubeconfig := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"config.kubeconfig\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(configFile, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\napiVersion: kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/v1\nkind: KubeSchedulerConfiguration\nclientConnection:\n  kubeconfig: '%s'\nleaderElection:\n  leaderElect: true`, configKubeconfig)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(configKubeconfig, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\napiVersion: v1\nkind: Config\nclusters:\n- cluster:\n    insecure-skip-tls-verify: true\n    server: %s\n  name: default\ncontexts:\n- context:\n    cluster: default\n    user: default\n  name: default\ncurrent-context: default\nusers:\n- name: default\n  user:\n    username: config\n`, server.URL)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\toldConfigFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"scheduler_old.yaml\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(oldConfigFile, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\napiVersion: componentconfig/v1alpha1\nkind: KubeSchedulerConfiguration\nclientConnection:\n  kubeconfig: '%s'\nleaderElection:\n  leaderElect: true`, configKubeconfig)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\tunknownVersionConfig := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"scheduler_invalid_wrong_api_version.yaml\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(unknownVersionConfig, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\napiVersion: kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/unknown\nkind: KubeSchedulerConfiguration\nclientConnection:\n  kubeconfig: '%s'\nleaderElection:\n  leaderElect: true`, configKubeconfig)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\tnoVersionConfig := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"scheduler_invalid_no_version.yaml\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(noVersionConfig, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\nkind: KubeSchedulerConfiguration\nclientConnection:\n  kubeconfig: '%s'\nleaderElection:\n  leaderElect: true`, configKubeconfig)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\tunknownFieldConfig := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"scheduler_invalid_unknown_field.yaml\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(unknownFieldConfig, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\napiVersion: kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/v1\nkind: KubeSchedulerConfiguration\nclientConnection:\n  kubeconfig: '%s'\nleaderElection:\n  leaderElect: true\nfoo: bar`, configKubeconfig)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\tduplicateFieldConfig := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"scheduler_invalid_duplicate_fields.yaml\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(duplicateFieldConfig, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\napiVersion: kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/v1\nkind: KubeSchedulerConfiguration\nclientConnection:\n  kubeconfig: '%s'\nleaderElection:\n  leaderElect: true\n  leaderElect: false`, configKubeconfig)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// flag-specified kubeconfig\n\tflagKubeconfig := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"flag.kubeconfig\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(flagKubeconfig, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\napiVersion: v1\nkind: Config\nclusters:\n- cluster:\n    insecure-skip-tls-verify: true\n    server: %s\n  name: default\ncontexts:\n- context:\n    cluster: default\n    user: default\n  name: default\ncurrent-context: default\nusers:\n- name: default\n  user:\n    username: flag\n`, server.URL)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// plugin config\n\tpluginConfigFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"plugin.yaml\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(pluginConfigFile, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\napiVersion: kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/v1\nkind: KubeSchedulerConfiguration\nclientConnection:\n  kubeconfig: '%s'\nprofiles:\n- plugins:\n    preEnqueue:\n      enabled:\n      - name: foo\n    reserve:\n      enabled:\n      - name: foo\n      - name: bar\n      disabled:\n      - name: VolumeBinding\n    preBind:\n      enabled:\n      - name: foo\n      disabled:\n      - name: VolumeBinding\n  pluginConfig:\n  - name: InterPodAffinity\n    args:\n      hardPodAffinityWeight: 2\n  - name: foo\n    args:\n      bar: baz\n`, configKubeconfig)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// multiple profiles config\n\tmultiProfilesConfig := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"multi-profiles.yaml\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(multiProfilesConfig, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\napiVersion: kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/v1\nkind: KubeSchedulerConfiguration\nclientConnection:\n  kubeconfig: '%s'\nprofiles:\n- schedulerName: \"foo-profile\"\n  plugins:\n    reserve:\n      enabled:\n      - name: foo\n      - name: VolumeBinding\n      disabled:\n      - name: VolumeBinding\n- schedulerName: \"bar-profile\"\n  plugins:\n    preBind:\n      disabled:\n      - name: VolumeBinding\n  pluginConfig:\n  - name: foo\n`, configKubeconfig)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// high throughput profile config\n\thighThroughputProfileConfig := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"high-throughput.yaml\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(highThroughputProfileConfig, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`\napiVersion: kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/v1\nkind: KubeSchedulerConfiguration\nclientConnection:\n  kubeconfig: '%s'\nprofiles:\n- schedulerName: \"high-throughput-profile\"\n  plugins:\n    preScore:\n      enabled:\n      - name: InterPodAffinity\n  pluginConfig:\n  - name: InterPodAffinity\n    args:\n      ignorePreferredTermsOfExistingPods: true\n`, configKubeconfig)), os.FileMode(0600)); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Insulate this test from picking up in-cluster config when run inside a pod\n\t// We can't assume we have permissions to write to /var/run/secrets/... from a unit test to mock in-cluster config for testing\n\tif len(os.Getenv(\"KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST\")) > 0 {\n\t\tt.Setenv(\"KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST\", \"\")\n\t}\n\n\tdefaultPodInitialBackoffSeconds := int64(1)\n\tdefaultPodMaxBackoffSeconds := int64(10)\n\tdefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore := ptr.To[int32](0)\n\n\ttestcases := []struct {\n\t\tname             string\n\t\toptions          *Options\n\t\texpectedUsername string\n\t\texpectedError    string\n\t\texpectedConfig   kubeschedulerconfig.KubeSchedulerConfiguration\n\t\tcheckErrFn       func(err error) bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"v1 config file\",\n\t\t\toptions: &Options{\n\t\t\t\tConfigFile: configFile,\n\t\t\t\tComponentConfig: func() *kubeschedulerconfig.KubeSchedulerConfiguration {\n\t\t\t\t\tcfg := configtesting.V1ToInternalWithDefaults(t, v1.KubeSchedulerConfiguration{})\n\t\t\t\t\treturn cfg\n\t\t\t\t}(),\n\t\t\t\tSecureServing: (&apiserveroptions.SecureServingOptions{\n\t\t\t\t\tServerCert: apiserveroptions.GeneratableKeyCert{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tCertDirectory: \"/a/b/c\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tPairName:      \"kube-scheduler\",\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\tHTTP2MaxStreamsPerConnection: 47,\n\t\t\t\t}).WithLoopback(),\n\t\t\t\tAuthentication: &apiserveroptions.DelegatingAuthenticationOptions{\n\t\t\t\t\tCacheTTL:   10 * time.Second,\n\t\t\t\t\tClientCert: apiserveroptions.ClientCertAuthenticationOptions{},\n\t\t\t\t\tRequestHeader: apiserveroptions.RequestHeaderAuthenticationOptions{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tUsernameHeaders:     []string{\"x-remote-user\"},\n\t\t\t\t\t\tGroupHeaders:        []string{\"x-remote-group\"},\n\t\t\t\t\t\tExtraHeaderPrefixes: []string{\"x-remote-extra-\"},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\tRemoteKubeConfigFileOptional: true,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tAuthorization: &apiserveroptions.DelegatingAuthorizationOptions{\n\t\t\t\t\tAllowCacheTTL:                10 * time.Second,\n\t\t\t\t\tDenyCacheTTL:                 10 * time.Second,\n\t\t\t\t\tRemoteKubeConfigFileOptional: true,\n\t\t\t\t\tAlwaysAllowPaths:             []string{\"/healthz\", \"/readyz\", \"/livez\"}, // note: this does not match /healthz/ or /healthz/*\n\t\t\t\t\tAlwaysAllowGroups:            []string{\"system:masters\"},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tLogs: logs.NewOptions(),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\texpectedUsername: \"config\",\n\t\t\texpectedConfig: kubeschedulerconfig.KubeSchedulerConfiguration{\n\t\t\t\tTypeMeta: metav1.TypeMeta{\n\t\t\t\t\tAPIVersion: v1.SchemeGroupVersion.String(),\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tParallelism: true,\n\t\t\t\t\tEnableContentionProfiling: true,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tLeaderElection: componentbaseconfig.LeaderElectionConfiguration{\n\t\t\t\t\tLeaderElect:       true,\n\t\t\t\t\tLeaseDuration: time.Second},\n\t\t\t\t\tResourceLock:      \"leases\",\n\t\t\t\t\tResourceNamespace: \"kube-system\",\n\t\t\t\t\tResourceName:      \"kube-scheduler\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tClientConnection: componentbaseconfig.ClientConnectionConfiguration{\n\t\t\t\t\tKubeconfig:  configKubeconfig,\n\t\t\t\t\tQPS:         50,\n\t\t\t\t\tBurst:       100,\n\t\t\t\t\tContentType: \"application/vnd.kubernetes.protobuf\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tPercentageOfNodesToScore: defaultPercentageOfNodesToScore,\n\t\t\t\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds: \\\"KubeSchedulerConfiguration\\\" is registered for version \\\"componentconfig/v1alpha1\\\"\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"unknown version kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/unknown\",\n\t\t\toptions: \\\"kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/unknown\\\"\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"config file with no version\",\n\t\t\toptions: &Options{\n\t\t\t\tConfigFile: noVersionConfig,\n\t\t\t\tLogs: tc.options.ComponentConfig == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif cfg, err := latest.Default(); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\ttc.options.ComponentConfig = cfg\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// create the config\n\t\t\t_, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\t\t\tconfig, err := tc.options.Config(ctx)\n\n\t\t\t// handle errors\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif tc.expectedError != \"\" || tc.checkErrFn != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tif tc.expectedError != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, err.Error(), tc.expectedError)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif tc.checkErrFn != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tassert.True(t, tc.checkErrFn(err), \"got error: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"unexpected error creating config: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif _, err := encodeConfig(&config.ComponentConfig); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"unexpected error in encodeConfig: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif diff := cmp.Diff(tc.expectedConfig, config.ComponentConfig); diff != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"incorrect config (-want,+got):\\n%s\", diff)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// ensure we have a client\n\t\t\tif config.Client == nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"unexpected nil client\")\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// test the client talks to the endpoint we expect with the credentials we expect\n\t\t\tusername = \"\"\n\t\t\t_, err = config.Client.Discovery().RESTClient().Get().AbsPath(\"/\").DoRaw(context.TODO())\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Error(err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif username != tc.expectedUsername {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected server call with user %q, got %q\", tc.expectedUsername, username)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n",

    "content": "\tnextStartNodeIndex int\n\n\t// logger *must* be initialized when creating a Scheduler,\n\t// otherwise logging functions will access a nil sink and\n\t// panic.\n\tlogger klog.Logger\n\n\t// registeredHandlers contains the registrations of all handlers. It's used to check if all handlers have finished syncing before the scheduling cycles start.\n\tregisteredHandlers []cache.ResourceEventHandlerRegistration\n}\n\nfunc (sched *Scheduler) applyDefaultHandlers() {\n\tsched.SchedulePod = sched.schedulePod\n\tsched.FailureHandler = sched.handleSchedulingFailure\n}\n\ntype schedulerOptions struct {\n\tcomponentConfigVersion string\n\tkubeConfig             *restclient.Config\n\t// Overridden by profile level percentageOfNodesToScore if set in v1.\n\tpercentageOfNodesToScore          int32\n\tpodInitialBackoffSeconds          int64\n\tpodMaxBackoffSeconds              int64\n\tpodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration time.Duration\n\t// Contains out-of-tree plugins to be merged with the in-tree registry.\n\tframeworkOutOfTreeRegistry frameworkruntime.Registry\n\tprofiles                   []schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerProfile\n\textenders                  []schedulerapi.Extender\n\tframeworkCapturer          FrameworkCapturer\n\tparallelism                int32\n\tapplyDefaultProfile        bool\n}\n\n// Option configures a Scheduler\ntype Option func(*schedulerOptions)\n\n// ScheduleResult represents the result of scheduling a pod.\ntype ScheduleResult struct {\n\t// Name of the selected node.\n\tSuggestedHost string\n\t// The number of nodes the scheduler evaluated the pod against in the filtering\n\t// phase and beyond.\n\t// Note that it contains the number of nodes that filtered out by PreFilterResult.\n\tEvaluatedNodes int\n\t// The number of nodes out of the evaluated ones that fit the pod.\n\tFeasibleNodes int\n\t// The nominating info for scheduling cycle.\n\tnominatingInfo *framework.NominatingInfo\n}\n\n// WithComponentConfigVersion sets the component config version to the\n// KubeSchedulerConfiguration version used. The string should be the full\n// scheme group/version of the external type we converted from (for example\n// \"kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/v1\")\nfunc WithComponentConfigVersion(apiVersion string) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.componentConfigVersion = apiVersion\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithKubeConfig sets the kube config for Scheduler.\nfunc WithKubeConfig(cfg *restclient.Config) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.kubeConfig = cfg\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithProfiles sets profiles for Scheduler. By default, there is one profile\n// with the name \"default-scheduler\".\nfunc WithProfiles(p ...schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerProfile) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.profiles = p\n\t\to.applyDefaultProfile = false\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithParallelism sets the parallelism for all scheduler algorithms. Default is 16.\nfunc WithParallelism(threads int32) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.parallelism = threads\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPercentageOfNodesToScore sets percentageOfNodesToScore for Scheduler.\n// The default value of 0 will use an adaptive percentage: 50 - (num of nodes)/125.\nfunc WithPercentageOfNodesToScore(percentageOfNodesToScore *int32) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\tif percentageOfNodesToScore != nil {\n\t\t\to.percentageOfNodesToScore = *percentageOfNodesToScore\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry sets the registry for out-of-tree plugins. Those plugins\n// will be appended to the default registry.\nfunc WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(registry frameworkruntime.Registry) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.frameworkOutOfTreeRegistry = registry\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPodInitialBackoffSeconds sets podInitialBackoffSeconds for Scheduler, the default value is 1\nfunc WithPodInitialBackoffSeconds(podInitialBackoffSeconds int64) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.podInitialBackoffSeconds = podInitialBackoffSeconds\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPodMaxBackoffSeconds sets podMaxBackoffSeconds for Scheduler, the default value is 10\nfunc WithPodMaxBackoffSeconds(podMaxBackoffSeconds int64) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.podMaxBackoffSeconds = podMaxBackoffSeconds\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration sets podMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration for PriorityQueue.\nfunc WithPodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration(duration time.Duration) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.podMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration = duration\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithExtenders sets extenders for the Scheduler\nfunc WithExtenders(e ...schedulerapi.Extender) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.extenders = e\n\t}\n}\n\n// FrameworkCapturer is used for registering a notify function in building framework.\ntype FrameworkCapturer func(schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerProfile)\n\n// WithBuildFrameworkCapturer sets a notify function for getting buildFramework details.\nfunc WithBuildFrameworkCapturer(fc FrameworkCapturer) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.frameworkCapturer = fc\n\t}\n}\n\nvar defaultSchedulerOptions = schedulerOptions{\n\tpercentageOfNodesToScore:          schedulerapi.DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore,\n\tpodInitialBackoffSeconds:          int64(internalqueue.DefaultPodInitialBackoffDuration.Seconds()),\n\tpodMaxBackoffSeconds:              int64(internalqueue.DefaultPodMaxBackoffDuration.Seconds()),\n\tpodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration: internalqueue.DefaultPodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration,\n\tparallelism:                       int32(parallelize.DefaultParallelism),\n\t// Ideally we would statically set the default profile here, but we can't because\n\t// creating the default profile may require testing feature gates, which may get\n\t// set dynamically in tests. Therefore, we delay creating it until New is actually\n\t// invoked.\n\tapplyDefaultProfile: true,",

  "originalFile": "/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage scheduler\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/dynamicinformer\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/informers\"\n\tcoreinformers \"k8s.io/client-go/informers/core/v1\"\n\tclientset \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n\trestclient \"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\tconfigv1 \"k8s.io/kube-scheduler/config/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\"\n\tschedulerapi \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/scheme\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/parallelize\"\n\tframeworkplugins \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/noderesources\"\n\tframeworkruntime \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/runtime\"\n\tinternalcache \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/internal/cache\"\n\tcachedebugger \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/internal/cache/debugger\"\n\tinternalqueue \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/internal/queue\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/metrics\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/profile\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\t// Duration the scheduler will wait before expiring an assumed pod.\n\t// See issue #106361 for more details about this parameter and its value.\n\tdurationToExpireAssumedPod time.Duration = 0\n)\n\n// ErrNoNodesAvailable is used to describe the error that no nodes available to schedule pods.\nvar ErrNoNodesAvailable = fmt.Errorf(\"no nodes available to schedule pods\")\n\n// Scheduler watches for new unscheduled pods. It attempts to find\n// nodes that they fit on and writes bindings back to the api server.\ntype Scheduler struct {\n\t// It is expected that changes made via Cache will be observed\n\t// by NodeLister and Algorithm.\n\tCache internalcache.Cache\n\n\tExtenders []framework.Extender\n\n\t// NextPod should be a function that blocks until the next pod\n\t// is available. We don't use a channel for this, because scheduling\n\t// a pod may take some amount of time and we don't want pods to get\n\t// stale while they sit in a channel.\n\tNextPod func(logger klog.Logger) (*framework.QueuedPodInfo, error)\n\n\t// FailureHandler is called upon a scheduling failure.\n\tFailureHandler FailureHandlerFn\n\n\t// SchedulePod tries to schedule the given pod to one of the nodes in the node list.\n\t// Return a struct of ScheduleResult with the name of suggested host on success,\n\t// otherwise will return a FitError with reasons.\n\tSchedulePod func(ctx context.Context, fwk framework.Framework, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (ScheduleResult, error)\n\n\t// Close this to shut down the scheduler.\n\tStopEverything <-chan struct{}\n\n\t// SchedulingQueue holds pods to be scheduled\n\tSchedulingQueue internalqueue.SchedulingQueue\n\n\t// Profiles are the scheduling profiles.\n\tProfiles profile.Map\n\n\tclient clientset.Interface\n\n\tnodeInfoSnapshot *internalcache.Snapshot\n\n\tpercentageOfNodesToScore int32\n\n\tnextStartNodeIndex int\n\n\t// logger *must* be initialized when creating a Scheduler,\n\t// otherwise logging functions will access a nil sink and\n\t// panic.\n\tlogger klog.Logger\n\n\t// registeredHandlers contains the registrations of all handlers. It's used to check if all handlers have finished syncing before the scheduling cycles start.\n\tregisteredHandlers []cache.ResourceEventHandlerRegistration\n}\n\nfunc (sched *Scheduler) applyDefaultHandlers() {\n\tsched.SchedulePod = sched.schedulePod\n\tsched.FailureHandler = sched.handleSchedulingFailure\n}\n\ntype schedulerOptions struct {\n\tcomponentConfigVersion string\n\tkubeConfig             *restclient.Config\n\t// Overridden by profile level percentageOfNodesToScore if set in v1.\n\tpercentageOfNodesToScore          int32\n\tpodInitialBackoffSeconds          int64\n\tpodMaxBackoffSeconds              int64\n\tpodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration time.Duration\n\t// Contains out-of-tree plugins to be merged with the in-tree registry.\n\tframeworkOutOfTreeRegistry frameworkruntime.Registry\n\tprofiles                   []schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerProfile\n\textenders                  []schedulerapi.Extender\n\tframeworkCapturer          FrameworkCapturer\n\tparallelism                int32\n\tapplyDefaultProfile        bool\n}\n\n// Option configures a Scheduler\ntype Option func(*schedulerOptions)\n\n// ScheduleResult represents the result of scheduling a pod.\ntype ScheduleResult struct {\n\t// Name of the selected node.\n\tSuggestedHost string\n\t// The number of nodes the scheduler evaluated the pod against in the filtering\n\t// phase and beyond.\n\t// Note that it contains the number of nodes that filtered out by PreFilterResult.\n\tEvaluatedNodes int\n\t// The number of nodes out of the evaluated ones that fit the pod.\n\tFeasibleNodes int\n\t// The nominating info for scheduling cycle.\n\tnominatingInfo *framework.NominatingInfo\n}\n\n// WithComponentConfigVersion sets the component config version to the\n// KubeSchedulerConfiguration version used. The string should be the full\n// scheme group/version of the external type we converted from (for example\n// \"kubescheduler.config.k8s.io/v1\")\nfunc WithComponentConfigVersion(apiVersion string) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.componentConfigVersion = apiVersion\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithKubeConfig sets the kube config for Scheduler.\nfunc WithKubeConfig(cfg *restclient.Config) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.kubeConfig = cfg\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithProfiles sets profiles for Scheduler. By default, there is one profile\n// with the name \"default-scheduler\".\nfunc WithProfiles(p ...schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerProfile) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.profiles = p\n\t\to.applyDefaultProfile = false\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithParallelism sets the parallelism for all scheduler algorithms. Default is 16.\nfunc WithParallelism(threads int32) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.parallelism = threads\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPercentageOfNodesToScore sets percentageOfNodesToScore for Scheduler.\n// The default value of 0 will use an adaptive percentage: 50 - (num of nodes)/125.\nfunc WithPercentageOfNodesToScore(percentageOfNodesToScore *int32) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\tif percentageOfNodesToScore != nil {\n\t\t\to.percentageOfNodesToScore = *percentageOfNodesToScore\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry sets the registry for out-of-tree plugins. Those plugins\n// will be appended to the default registry.\nfunc WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(registry frameworkruntime.Registry) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.frameworkOutOfTreeRegistry = registry\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPodInitialBackoffSeconds sets podInitialBackoffSeconds for Scheduler, the default value is 1\nfunc WithPodInitialBackoffSeconds(podInitialBackoffSeconds int64) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.podInitialBackoffSeconds = podInitialBackoffSeconds\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPodMaxBackoffSeconds sets podMaxBackoffSeconds for Scheduler, the default value is 10\nfunc WithPodMaxBackoffSeconds(podMaxBackoffSeconds int64) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.podMaxBackoffSeconds = podMaxBackoffSeconds\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration sets podMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration for PriorityQueue.\nfunc WithPodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration(duration time.Duration) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.podMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration = duration\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithExtenders sets extenders for the Scheduler\nfunc WithExtenders(e ...schedulerapi.Extender) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.extenders = e\n\t}\n}\n\n// FrameworkCapturer is used for registering a notify function in building framework.\ntype FrameworkCapturer func(schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerProfile)\n\n// WithBuildFrameworkCapturer sets a notify function for getting buildFramework details.\nfunc WithBuildFrameworkCapturer(fc FrameworkCapturer) Option {\n\treturn func(o *schedulerOptions) {\n\t\to.frameworkCapturer = fc\n\t}\n}\n\nvar defaultSchedulerOptions = schedulerOptions{\n\tpercentageOfNodesToScore:          schedulerapi.DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore,\n\tpodInitialBackoffSeconds:          int64(internalqueue.DefaultPodInitialBackoffDuration.Seconds()),\n\tpodMaxBackoffSeconds:              int64(internalqueue.DefaultPodMaxBackoffDuration.Seconds()),\n\tpodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration: internalqueue.DefaultPodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration,\n\tparallelism:                       int32(parallelize.DefaultParallelism),\n\t// Ideally we would statically set the default profile here, but we can't because\n\t// creating the default profile may require testing feature gates, which may get\n\t// set dynamically in tests. Therefore, we delay creating it until New is actually\n\t// invoked.\n\tapplyDefaultProfile: true,\n}\n\n// New returns a Scheduler\nfunc New(ctx context.Context,\n\tclient clientset.Interface,\n\tinformerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory,\n\tdynInformerFactory dynamicinformer.DynamicSharedInformerFactory,\n\trecorderFactory profile.RecorderFactory,\n\topts ...Option) (*Scheduler, error) {\n\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tstopEverything := ctx.Done()\n\n\toptions := defaultSchedulerOptions\n\tfor _, opt := range opts {\n\t\topt(&options)\n\t}\n\n\tif options.applyDefaultProfile {\n\t\tvar versionedCfg configv1.KubeSchedulerConfiguration\n\t\tscheme.Scheme.Default(&versionedCfg)\n\t\tcfg := schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerConfiguration{}\n\t\tif err := scheme.Scheme.Convert(&versionedCfg, &cfg, nil); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\toptions.profiles = cfg.Profiles\n\t}\n\n\tregistry := frameworkplugins.NewInTreeRegistry()\n\tif err := registry.Merge(options.frameworkOutOfTreeRegistry); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.Register()\n\n\textenders, err := buildExtenders(logger, options.extenders, options.profiles)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"couldn't build extenders: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tpodLister := informerFactory.Core().V1().Pods().Lister()\n\tnodeLister := informerFactory.Core().V1().Nodes().Lister()\n\n\tsnapshot := internalcache.NewEmptySnapshot()\n\tmetricsRecorder := metrics.NewMetricsAsyncRecorder(1000, time.Second, stopEverything)\n\n\tprofiles, err := profile.NewMap(ctx, options.profiles, registry, recorderFactory,\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithComponentConfigVersion(options.componentConfigVersion),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithClientSet(client),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithKubeConfig(options.kubeConfig),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithInformerFactory(informerFactory),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithSnapshotSharedLister(snapshot),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithCaptureProfile(frameworkruntime.CaptureProfile(options.frameworkCapturer)),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithParallelism(int(options.parallelism)),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithExtenders(extenders),\n\t\tframeworkruntime.WithMetricsRecorder(metricsRecorder),\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"initializing profiles: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(profiles) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"at least one profile is required\")\n\t}\n\n\tpreEnqueuePluginMap := make(map[string][]framework.PreEnqueuePlugin)\n\tqueueingHintsPerProfile := make(internalqueue.QueueingHintMapPerProfile)\n\tfor profileName, profile := range profiles {\n\t\tpreEnqueuePluginMap[profileName] = profile.PreEnqueuePlugins()\n\t\tqueueingHintsPerProfile[profileName] = buildQueueingHintMap(profile.EnqueueExtensions())\n\t}\n\n\tpodQueue := internalqueue.NewSchedulingQueue(\n\t\tprofiles[options.profiles[0].SchedulerName].QueueSortFunc(),\n\t\tinformerFactory,\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPodInitialBackoffDuration(time.Duration(options.podInitialBackoffSeconds)*time.Second),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPodMaxBackoffDuration(time.Duration(options.podMaxBackoffSeconds)*time.Second),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPodLister(podLister),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPodMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration(options.podMaxInUnschedulablePodsDuration),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPreEnqueuePluginMap(preEnqueuePluginMap),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithQueueingHintMapPerProfile(queueingHintsPerProfile),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithPluginMetricsSamplePercent(pluginMetricsSamplePercent),\n\t\tinternalqueue.WithMetricsRecorder(*metricsRecorder),\n\t)\n\n\tfor _, fwk := range profiles {\n\t\tfwk.SetPodNominator(podQueue)\n\t}\n\n\tschedulerCache := internalcache.New(ctx, durationToExpireAssumedPod)\n\n\t// Setup cache debugger.\n\tdebugger := cachedebugger.New(nodeLister, podLister, schedulerCache, podQueue)\n\tdebugger.ListenForSignal(ctx)\n\n\tsched := &Scheduler{\n\t\tCache:                    schedulerCache,\n\t\tclient:                   client,\n\t\tnodeInfoSnapshot:         snapshot,\n\t\tpercentageOfNodesToScore: options.percentageOfNodesToScore,\n\t\tExtenders:                extenders,\n\t\tStopEverything:           stopEverything,\n\t\tSchedulingQueue:          podQueue,\n\t\tProfiles:                 profiles,\n\t\tlogger:                   logger,\n\t}\n\tsched.NextPod = podQueue.Pop\n\tsched.applyDefaultHandlers()\n\n\tif err = addAllEventHandlers(sched, informerFactory, dynInformerFactory, unionedGVKs(queueingHintsPerProfile)); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"adding event handlers: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn sched, nil\n}\n\n// defaultQueueingHintFn is the default queueing hint function.\n// It always returns Queue as the queueing hint.\nvar defaultQueueingHintFn = func(_ klog.Logger, _ *v1.Pod, _, _ interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n}\n\nfunc buildQueueingHintMap(es []framework.EnqueueExtensions) internalqueue.QueueingHintMap {\n\tqueueingHintMap := make(internalqueue.QueueingHintMap)\n\tfor _, e := range es {\n\t\tevents := e.EventsToRegister()\n\n\t\t// This will happen when plugin registers with empty events, it's usually the case a pod\n\t\t// will become reschedulable only for self-update, e.g. schedulingGates plugin, the pod\n\t\t// will enter into the activeQ via priorityQueue.Update().\n\t\tif len(events) == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Note: Rarely, a plugin implements EnqueueExtensions but returns nil.\n\t\t// We treat it as: the plugin is not interested in any event, and hence pod failed by that plugin\n\t\t// cannot be moved by any regular cluster event.\n\t\t// So, we can just ignore such EventsToRegister here.\n\n\t\tregisterNodeAdded := false\n\t\tregisterNodeTaintUpdated := false\n\t\tfor _, event := range events {\n\t\t\tfn := event.QueueingHintFn\n\t\t\tif fn == nil || !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.SchedulerQueueingHints) {\n\t\t\t\tfn = defaultQueueingHintFn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif event.Event.Resource == framework.Node {\n\t\t\t\tif event.Event.ActionType&framework.Add != 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tregisterNodeAdded = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif event.Event.ActionType&framework.UpdateNodeTaint != 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tregisterNodeTaintUpdated = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tqueueingHintMap[event.Event] = append(queueingHintMap[event.Event], &internalqueue.QueueingHintFunction{\n\t\t\t\tPluginName:     e.Name(),\n\t\t\t\tQueueingHintFn: fn,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t\tif registerNodeAdded && !registerNodeTaintUpdated {\n\t\t\t// Temporally fix for the issue https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/109437\n\t\t\t// NodeAdded QueueingHint isn't always called because of preCheck.\n\t\t\t// It's definitely not something expected for plugin developers,\n\t\t\t// and registering UpdateNodeTaint event is the only mitigation for now.\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// So, here registers UpdateNodeTaint event for plugins that has NodeAdded event, but don't have UpdateNodeTaint event.\n\t\t\t// It has a bad impact for the requeuing efficiency though, a lot better than some Pods being stuch in the\n\t\t\t// unschedulable pod pool.\n\t\t\t// This behavior will be removed when we remove the preCheck feature.\n\t\t\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n\t\t\tqueueingHintMap[framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.UpdateNodeTaint}] =\n\t\t\t\tappend(queueingHintMap[framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.UpdateNodeTaint}],\n\t\t\t\t\t&internalqueue.QueueingHintFunction{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tPluginName:     e.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tQueueingHintFn: defaultQueueingHintFn,\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn queueingHintMap\n}\n\n// Run begins watching and scheduling. It starts scheduling and blocked until the context is done.\nfunc (sched *Scheduler) Run(ctx context.Context) {\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tsched.SchedulingQueue.Run(logger)\n\n\t// We need to start scheduleOne loop in a dedicated goroutine,\n\t// because scheduleOne function hangs on getting the next item\n\t// from the SchedulingQueue.\n\t// If there are no new pods to schedule, it will be hanging there\n\t// and if done in this goroutine it will be blocking closing\n\t// SchedulingQueue, in effect causing a deadlock on shutdown.\n\tgo wait.UntilWithContext(ctx, sched.ScheduleOne, 0)\n\n\t<-ctx.Done()\n\tsched.SchedulingQueue.Close()\n\n\t// If the plugins satisfy the io.Closer interface, they are closed.\n\terr := sched.Profiles.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to close plugins\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewInformerFactory creates a SharedInformerFactory and initializes a scheduler specific\n// in-place podInformer.\nfunc NewInformerFactory(cs clientset.Interface, resyncPeriod time.Duration) informers.SharedInformerFactory {\n\tinformerFactory := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(cs, resyncPeriod)\n\tinformerFactory.InformerFor(&v1.Pod{}, newPodInformer)\n\treturn informerFactory\n}\n\nfunc buildExtenders(logger klog.Logger, extenders []schedulerapi.Extender, profiles []schedulerapi.KubeSchedulerProfile) ([]framework.Extender, error) {\n\tvar fExtenders []framework.Extender\n\tif len(extenders) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar ignoredExtendedResources []string\n\tvar ignorableExtenders []framework.Extender\n\tfor i := range extenders {\n\t\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Creating extender\", \"extender\", extenders[i])\n\t\textender, err := NewHTTPExtender(&extenders[i])\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !extender.IsIgnorable() {\n\t\t\tfExtenders = append(fExtenders, extender)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tignorableExtenders = append(ignorableExtenders, extender)\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, r := range extenders[i].ManagedResources {\n\t\t\tif r.IgnoredByScheduler {\n\t\t\t\tignoredExtendedResources = append(ignoredExtendedResources, r.Name)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// place ignorable extenders to the tail of extenders\n\tfExtenders = append(fExtenders, ignorableExtenders...)\n\n\t// If there are any extended resources found from the Extenders, append them to the pluginConfig for each profile.\n\t// This should only have an effect on ComponentConfig, where it is possible to configure Extenders and\n\t// plugin args (and in which case the extender ignored resources take precedence).\n\tif len(ignoredExtendedResources) == 0 {\n\t\treturn fExtenders, nil\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := range profiles {\n\t\tprof := &profiles[i]\n\t\tvar found = false\n\t\tfor k := range prof.PluginConfig {\n\t\t\tif prof.PluginConfig[k].Name == noderesources.Name {\n\t\t\t\t// Update the existing args\n\t\t\t\tpc := &prof.PluginConfig[k]\n\t\t\t\targs, ok := pc.Args.(*schedulerapi.NodeResourcesFitArgs)\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"want args to be of type NodeResourcesFitArgs, got %T\", pc.Args)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\targs.IgnoredResources = ignoredExtendedResources\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"can't find NodeResourcesFitArgs in plugin config\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn fExtenders, nil\n}\n\ntype FailureHandlerFn func(ctx context.Context, fwk framework.Framework, podInfo *framework.QueuedPodInfo, status *framework.Status, nominatingInfo *framework.NominatingInfo, start time.Time)\n\nfunc unionedGVKs(queueingHintsPerProfile internalqueue.QueueingHintMapPerProfile) map[framework.GVK]framework.ActionType {\n\tgvkMap := make(map[framework.GVK]framework.ActionType)\n\tfor _, queueingHints := range queueingHintsPerProfile {\n\t\tfor evt := range queueingHints {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := gvkMap[evt.Resource]; ok {\n\t\t\t\tgvkMap[evt.Resource] |= evt.ActionType\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tgvkMap[evt.Resource] = evt.ActionType\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn gvkMap\n}\n\n// newPodInformer creates a shared index informer that returns only non-terminal pods.\n// The PodInformer allows indexers to be added, but note that only non-conflict indexers are allowed.\nfunc newPodInformer(cs clientset.Interface, resyncPeriod time.Duration) cache.SharedIndexInformer {\n\tselector := fmt.Sprintf(\"status.phase!=%v,status.phase!=%v\", v1.PodSucceeded, v1.PodFailed)\n\ttweakListOptions := func(options *metav1.ListOptions) {\n\t\toptions.FieldSelector = selector\n\t}\n\tinformer := coreinformers.NewFilteredPodInformer(cs, metav1.NamespaceAll, resyncPeriod, cache.Indexers{}, tweakListOptions)\n\n\t// Dropping `.metadata.managedFields` to improve memory usage.\n\t// The Extract workflow (i.e. `ExtractPod`) should be unused.\n\ttrim := func(obj interface{}) (interface{}, error) {\n\t\tif accessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err == nil {\n\t\t\taccessor.SetManagedFields(nil)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn obj, nil\n\t}\n\tinformer.SetTransform(trim)\n\treturn informer\n}\n",

1. EDIT /workspace/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/types.go
Before
// KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.
type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {
	// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.
	// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's "spec.schedulerName", then the pod
	// is scheduled with this profile.
	SchedulerName string

	// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible
	// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in
	// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find
	// at least "minFeasibleNodesToFind" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.
	// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,
	// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.
	// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the
	// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,
	// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.
	PercentageOfNodesToScore *int32

	// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.
	// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the
	// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that
	// should be disabled.
	// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,
	// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.
	// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and
	// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.
	Plugins *Plugins

	// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.
	// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config
	// for th
... (truncated)
After
// KubeSchedulingProfile is a scheduling profile.
type KubeSchedulingProfile struct {
	// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.
	// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's "spec.schedulerName", then the pod
	// is scheduled with this profile.
	SchedulerName string

	// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible
	// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in
	// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find
	// at least "minFeasibleNodesToFind" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.
	// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,
	// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.
	// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the
	// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,
	// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.
	PercentageOfNodesToScore *int32

	// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.
	// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the
	// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that
	// should be disabled.
	// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,
	// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.
	// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and
	// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.

2. EDIT /workspace/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/types.go
Before
	// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can
	// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated
	// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled
	// with the "default-scheduler" profile, if present here.
	Profiles []KubeSchedulerProfile
After
	// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can
	// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated
	// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled
	// with the "default-scheduler" profile, if present here.
	Profiles []KubeSchedulingProfile

4. EDIT /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/kube-scheduler/config/v1/types.go
Before
// KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.
type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {
	// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.
	// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's "spec.schedulerName", then the pod
	// is scheduled with this profile.
	SchedulerName *string `json:"schedulerName,omitempty"`

	// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible
	// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in
	// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find
	// at least "minFeasibleNodesToFind" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.
	// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,
	// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.
	// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the
	// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,
	// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.
	PercentageOfNodesToScore *int32 `json:"percentageOfNodesToScore,omitempty"`

	// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.
	// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the
	// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that
	// should be disabled.
	// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,
	// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.
	// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and
	// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.
	Plugins *Plugins `json:"plugins,omitempty"`

	// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments fo
... (truncated)
After
// KubeSchedulingProfile is a scheduling profile.
type KubeSchedulingProfile struct {
	// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.
	// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's "spec.schedulerName", then the pod
	// is scheduled with this profile.
	SchedulerName *string `json:"schedulerName,omitempty"`

	// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible
	// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in
	// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find
	// at least "minFeasibleNodesToFind" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.
	// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,
	// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.
	// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the
	// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,
	// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.
	PercentageOfNodesToScore *int32 `json:"percentageOfNodesToScore,omitempty"`

	// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.
	// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the
	// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that
	// should be disabled.
	// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,
	// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.
	// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and
	// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.
	Plugins *Plugins `json:"plugins,omitempty"`

	// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments 
... (truncated)
